comments.text:
## Background

Currently, `PersistencePhase` builds the entire system transaction by pulling data from `recovery_attempt` and `transaction_system_layout`. This creates indirection where phases set state, then PersistencePhase reads it back and builds mutations.

For example, the shard_keys flow:
1. `MaterializerBootstrapPhase` determines `shard_layout`
2. `TopologyPhase` copies it to `transaction_system_layout.shard_layout`
3. `PersistencePhase` reads from TSL and calls `build_shard_keys()`

This indirection led to the "Empty shard_keys table" bug (fixed in `fresh_cluster?`).

## Proposed Refactor

Thread a `Tx` (write-only transaction) through recovery phases. Each phase adds its own mutations directly:

```elixir
# MaterializerBootstrapPhase
def execute(recovery_attempt, context) do
  shard_layout = determine_shard_layout(...)
  
  tx = recovery_attempt.pending_tx
  |> build_shard_keys(shard_layout)
  
  {%{recovery_attempt | pending_tx: tx, shard_layout: shard_layout}, NextPhase}
end
```

## Benefits

- Each phase owns its mutations - logic lives with data source
- Eliminates risk of PersistencePhase forgetting to build keys
- Removes indirection through TSL for write-only data
- Clearer responsibility boundaries

## Considerations

- Need to handle phase retry (reset Tx or ensure idempotency)
- May want to keep some state in recovery_attempt for other purposes (validation, etc.)
- PersistencePhase becomes simpler - just submits the accumulated Tx

## Files to Modify

- `lib/bedrock/control_plane/config/recovery_attempt.ex` - Add `pending_tx` field
- `lib/bedrock/control_plane/director/recovery/initialization_phase.ex` - Initialize Tx, add shard_keys
- `lib/bedrock/control_plane/director/recovery/materializer_bootstrap_phase.ex` - Add shard_keys for existing clusters
- `lib/bedrock/control_plane/director/recovery/topology_phase.ex` - Add layout keys
- `lib/bedrock/control_plane/director/recovery/persistence_phase.ex` - Simplify to just submit Tx
comments.author: Jason Allum

comments.text: Audit note: Mike's branch heavily refactored recovery phases â€” removed obsolete phases, reworked TopologyPhase/LockingPhase, moved durable_version calc to LogRecoveryPlanningPhase. PersistencePhase still exists but the indirection described here may be partially resolved. Needs review before starting work.

title: Refactor: Thread Tx through recovery phases instead of building in PersistencePhase
